headline: Core contracts are verified and public, backed by extensive recent audits from top-tier firms.

findings.text: The Etherscan block explorer confirms that the source code for the deployed Aave V3 Pool contract and the Pool Configurator V3 proxy are verified.

The protocol maintains an extensive, publicly accessible repository of security audits covering all minor version upgrades. The most recent V3.6 and V4 audits were completed between November 2025 and February 2026.
Audits are performed by highly recognized security firms in the Ethereum ecosystem, including Certora, OpenZeppelin, Trail of Bits, SigmaPrime, and PeckShield.

The most recent smart contract security assessments occurred within 6 months of this review (November 2025 - February 2026), indicating that the currently deployed codebase has minimal to no unaudited post-audit drift.

steelman.red: The protocol operates entirely closed-source contracts that cannot be scrutinized on block explorers, preventing independent verification of its logic.
steelman.orange: While some core contracts are verified, the protocol's latest proxy implementations lack verification or have drifted significantly since their last security audit.
steelman.green: The deployed bytecode is fully verified on block explorers, corresponds to public GitHub repositories, and is continuously audited by top-tier security firms with no stale post-audit drift.

rationale.verdict: Choosing green because the deployed core contracts are verified on Etherscan, the open-source repository is publicly maintained, and the protocol has a highly robust, up-to-date auditing track record from recognized tier-1 security firms as recently as February 2026.

evidence.shows: Verified source code and active transactions for the Aave V3 Pool contract on Ethereum.
Verified source code for the Aave V3 Pool Configurator proxy contract on Ethereum.
The public GitHub repository containing the actively maintained Aave v3.6 origin codebase.
The official Aave security portal listing extensive audits up to V3.6 (Nov 2025) and V4 (Feb 2026).
Official documentation covering the architecture and repository structure of the protocol's core components.

unknowns: V2: did not independently compile the repository to run a strict bytecode equivalence diff against the deployed contracts
V6: did not manually trace proxy slots to explicitly verify the source tabs of the corresponding implementation contracts

protocol_metadata.about: Aave V3 is a decentralized non-custodial liquidity markets protocol where users can participate as suppliers or borrowers. Suppliers provide liquidity to earn passive yield, while borrowers can take out loans in an overcollateralized or undercollateralized fashion. The protocol features isolated lending pools, dual borrowing modes, and is actively managed by Aave DAO governance.

headline: Core contracts verified on explorers with exact-match implementation; official repo corresponds; recognized-firm audits cover V3.6

findings.text: Ethereum Pool proxy 0x87870Bca3F3fD6335C3F4ce8392D69350B4fA4E2 verified as InitializableImmutableAdminUpgradeabilityProxy (standard pattern, Source Code Verified); implementation 0x8147b99df7672a21809c9093e6f6ce1a60f119bd verified exactly as PoolInstance (v0.8.27, Exact Match). PoolAddressesProvider 0x2f39d218133AFaB8F2B819B1066c7E434Ad94E9e verified standalone (v0.8.10). Similar-match proxy pattern per V1 guidance does not count as gap.

Explorer-visible source for proxy (InitializableImmutableAdminUpgradeabilityProxy) and implementation (PoolInstance) corresponds in structure, file layout, and contract logic to https://github.com/aave-dao/aave-v3-origin (v3.6 codebase, Foundry-based, src/contracts/protocol/ directories). Official docs explicitly link this repo as source of truth. No commit SHA pinned or bytecode diff executed.

https://aave.com/security lists 15+ audit reports scoped to Aave V3 / V3.6 (e.g. Nov 2025 Security Review, Security Assessment & Formal Verification Report, Jul 2025 Smart Contract Audit Report) plus earlier V3.0–V3.5; GitHub repo https://github.com/aave-dao/aave-v3-origin/audits/ contains the reports. Scope includes core protocol contracts matching current deployments.

Audits include Certora (formal verification, explicitly recognized) and MixBytes (recognized per checklist); additional reviews by Blackthorn, Savant, Pashov noted in repo audits folder. No unknown-firm-only posture.

Latest V3.6 audits dated Nov 2025; v3.6.0 repo release Jan 2026; current on-chain state Apr 2026 implies ~5-month window. No source signals non-trivial post-audit changes (new functions, access-control modifications, or accounting alterations) requiring mandatory re-audit; continuous audit cadence and formal verification mitigate drift risk.
Proxy contract verified on Etherscan; implementation contract independently verified on separate explorer page (exact bytecode match, not partial). No unverified implementation behind verified proxy.

steelman.red: Custom upgradeability proxy pattern plus 5-month gap between Nov 2025 V3.6 audits and Apr 2026 deployment without pinned commit or explicit re-audit confirmation could indicate undetected drift or incomplete coverage, warranting red if any single major contract lacked verification.
steelman.orange: Full verification of proxy and implementation is present and repo correspondence holds, yet absence of exact deployed commit SHA, no bytecode diff, and audits predating the v3.6.0 tag by months introduce measurable uncertainty on precise audit-to-deployment alignment, supporting orange.
steelman.green: Ethereum core contracts (Pool proxy + impl, PoolAddressesProvider) show verified bytecode with exact-match implementation, direct structural correspondence to the canonical public repo https://github.com/aave-dao/aave-v3-origin, and coverage by multiple recognized-firm audits (Certora formal verification, MixBytes) within a 5-month window with no flagged post-audit modifications, satisfying all green criteria.

rationale.verdict: Choosing green because verified proxy+implementation on Etherscan (exact match for impl), direct repo correspondence, and recognized-firm audits with recent V3.6 scope and acceptable drift together outweigh the conservative unknowns around unpinned commit and exact PDF retrieval; this is the strongest of the three steel-mans given concrete explorer and GitHub evidence.

evidence.shows: Pool V3 proxy contract is Source Code Verified as InitializableImmutableAdminUpgradeabilityProxy; lists implementation address; standard proxy pattern confirmed; fetched during run

Pool implementation contract is Source Code Verified (Exact Match) as PoolInstance (v0.8.27+commit.40a35a09); constructor args reference provider 0x2f39d218133AFaB8F2B819B1066c7E434Ad94E9e; fetched during run
PoolAddressesProvider V3 is Source Code Verified standalone (v0.8.10); owner set at deployment; fetched during run

Canonical source repo for Aave v3.6 (latest release v3.6.0 Jan 2026); contains src/contracts matching explorer-visible Pool logic, PoolAddressesProvider, and upgradeability patterns; audits/ folder present; fetched during run

Lists 15+ audit reports scoped to Aave V3/V3.6 (Nov 2025 formal verification & security reviews, Jul 2025 audits, earlier V3 versions); links to GitHub contract repos for each; supports audit coverage and recognized-firm posture via repo cross-reference; fetched during run
Official Solidity/JS registry providing verified addresses for AaveV3Ethereum.POOL etc. across chains; confirms Ethereum Pool at 0x87870Bca3F3fD6335C3F4ce8392D69350B4fA4E2; fetched during run
Canonical deployed-contracts dashboard with Ethereum hub/spoke/peripheral addresses and direct Etherscan links; fetched during run

unknowns: V2: exact commit SHA (full 40-char) matching the deployed implementation bytecode not identified or pinned; no independent compile or bytecode diff performed between explorer artifact and repo (scope limit, not downgrade)

V3: specific auditor firm names, exact PDF report URLs, and per-contract scope details for the Nov 2025 V3.6 reports not extracted from security page (titles and GitHub scopes only); exact drift comparison to deployed commit unavailable

protocol_metadata.about: Aave V3 is a non-custodial liquidity protocol on 20+ EVM chains where users supply assets to earn yield or post collateral to borrow other assets. It uses isolated lending pools, efficiency mode (eMode) for correlated-asset optimization, and cross-chain portals. Governance via AAVE token votes enables parameter changes and contract upgrades through timelocks, with continuous security reviews distinguishing its mature, high-liquidity design.
